title: Disability Related Parenting Programs - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Parenting & Childcare > Disability Related Parenting Programs 51 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Autism Spectrum Disorder Deafness Veteran/Military Personnel Issues ARC LANE COUNTY FAMILIES CONNECTED/FAMILIAS EN CONEXION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides peer support to parents who are raising a child with an intellectual and/or developmental disability and plan for a positive future. Helps families build community connections and connects families to events, information, and resources. 4181 E Street Springfield, OR 97478 Eligibility: Familes with children 0-21 with intellectual or developmental disabilities Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm, some events occur on the weekends Email (541) 343-5256 124 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BENTON COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH BARBARA ROSS BUILDING DEVELOPMENTAL DIVERSITY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a range of support services to individuals experiencing intellectual and/or developmental disabilities. Applicant must be an Oregon resident and a U.S. citizen. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (541) 523-8366 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON FAMILY TO FAMILY HEALTH INFORMATION CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Operates a call-in center for family members of children and youth with special needs and chronic health conditions (developmental and emotional disabilities, behavioral problems, and chronic physical illnesses) and professionals who serve them. The center specializes in providing information and resources related to health information, health care access, and special needs systems and supports. All calls are confidential. Families may also request to be placed on an email list to receive monthly correspondence about issues of importance to children and youth with special needs in Oregon. 707 SW Gaines Street Portland, OR 97239 Eligibility: Family members of individuals age 26 and younger with special physical, emotional, behavioral, or mental health needs, including those who are at risk or suspected of having a special need. The child/youth does not have to have a diagnosed condition. Does not require documentation to prove citizenship to receive services. Hours: Message line available 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (833) 990-9930 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PEARL BUCK CENTER PEARL BUCK PRESCHOOL AND FAMILY SUPPORTS PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides case management, resource referral, support, and advocacy services to students whose parents have borderline disabilities but are not eligible for state developmental disabilities services. Parenting support is offered through home visits and regular phone contact by the teacher or parent navigator. Also provides preschool services to children with average to above-average intelligence whose parents have a cognitive limitation. Meals and transportation are available for the half-day program. Transportation is offered for those in Eugene and Springfield. 3690 W 1st Avenue Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Families with children and parents with cognitive differences. Preschool ages 2-5.
